text: Quality higher education is a pivotal contributor to the socio–economic development of a nation. Education service quality is one of the important variables for measuring the quality of higher education. Hence, this study aimed at identifying the persisting challenges in Ethiopian higher education. Using qualitative research design, the study employed a data collection instrument of document analysis, focus group discussions and interviews. A total of 89 research papers which were related to the expansion of higher education in Ethiopia and its quality, were comprehensively reviewed. During the reviewing process, various government policy documents, peer reviewed articles, PhD dissertations and empirical studies which were closely related to the themes of the study were thoroughly consulted. Generally, the findings of the study revealed that despite the numerous initiatives which have been taken for enhancing the quality of higher education in Ethiopia, the efforts yield a minimal success for improving the quality of higher education. For instance, the experienced teaching staff, adequate supply of ICT infrastructures, libraries and laboratory and workshop materials, adequate dormitory facilities and enough classes are not sufficiently provided in most higher educational institutions in the country. Hence, it could conclude that the majority of higher education institutions in Ethiopia have delivered poor educational services to students. Based on the findings and conclusions of the study, recommendations were made.
